name: Lionel Hampton
description: '''Lionel Leo Hampton''' (April 20, 1908 – August 31, 2002) was an American [[jazz]] [[Vibraphone|vibraphonist]], [[Percussion instrument|percussionist]], and bandleader. He worked with jazz musicians from [[Teddy Wilson]], [[Benny Goodman]], and [[Buddy Rich]] to [[Charlie Parker]], [[Charles Mingus]], and [[Quincy Jones]]. In 1992, he was inducted into the [[Alabama Jazz Hall of Fame]], and he was awarded the [[National Medal of Arts]] in 1996.
